title: Crucible Experiment Manifest (experiment.v1)
description: The declarative experiment manifest — the user-facing input contract of the Crucible harness (crucible_ux_output_plan_260708.md §3.1, ratified 2026-07-08; Phase A, config#1966). An experiment is DECLARED, not coded: each slot (R research / M model / S strategy) independently resolves to the stock reference implementation, a user artifact/command (R/M, validated against signals.v1 / predictions.v1), or a Python entry point (S, validated by the Slot S conformance kit). Deliberately NOT a DSL: no templating, no inheritance — it names things that exist and binds slots. Tuned beliefs (thresholds, prompts, weights) stay in private experiment bundles per the harness/experiment line (ARCHITECTURE §33); this manifest only declares WHICH experiment runs. Evolution is ADDITIVE-ONLY: new optional fields may appear at any time (additionalProperties stays true); removing or renaming a required field is a breaking change requiring a v2 schema.

schema_version.description: Manifest contract version stamp (required from birth — this contract has no pre-versioned legacy artifacts)

id.description: Slug identity; becomes the experiments/{id}/ S3 namespace at M3

universe.description: Named universe from the data platform (e.g. sp500_400)

slots.description: Per-slot implementation binding. Absent slot key = stock.

horizons.description: HorizonPolicy names — a parameter, never a column suffix (ARCH §48)

snapshot.description: PIT pin: 'latest' or a dated snapshot id; a dated id reproduces a run exactly

artifact_slot.description: Slot R/M binding — an artifact contract (signals.v1 / predictions.v1): stock, a pre-produced artifact path, or a command the harness invokes then validates.

code_slot.description: Slot S binding — a code contract (ExitRule protocol via the alpha_engine.exit_rules entry-point group; conformance = the Slot S kit C1–C5).
